mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS path lowers discount rates and raises equity valuation multiples, so lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when inflation is stable or falling.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward S&P 500 returns are not positive on average after FEDFUNDS declines, or if the sign flips and returns become negative during easing periods even when CPIAUCSL is flat-to-lower.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above its recent trend indicates labor-market deterioration and future earnings risk, so the S&P 500 should have negative 20-day forward returns after a clear unemployment upshift.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations show positive 20d SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the signal loses directional value when PAYEMS is not simultaneously weakening.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in RSAFS and HOUST signals broad demand softening across consumption and housing, which should depress near-term risk appetite and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-deterioration condition does not improve short-horizon hit rate versus either series alone, or if 5d returns are not negative in triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: Falling INDPRO with elevated UNRATE signals a supply-side output slowdown that weakens cyclical earnings expectations, so the S&P 500 should underperform over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average while UNRATE is above 9%
failure_condition: Reject if the combined slowdown regime does not produce negative 60d returns or if the signal is indistinguishable from random once the unemployment condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is near the zero lower bound and CPIAUCSL remains subdued, further monetary accommodation is interpreted as supportive liquidity for equities, so the S&P 500 should rise over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.50% and CPIAUCSL inflation trend is below its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if near-zero rates combined with subdued inflation do not precede positive 20d SPX returns, or if the effect disappears once FEDFUNDS is already at the lower bound.

mechanism_narrative: A positive surprise in GDPC1 after a prior slowdown marks renewed real-demand momentum, which should lift earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns positive after at least one negative quarter
failure_condition: Reject if post-slowdown GDP reacceleration fails to improve 60d SPX returns versus baseline, or if positive GDP turns are followed by negative equity performance more often than not.

mechanism_narrative: An improvement in PAYEMS combined with a falling UNRATE indicates broadening labor income and consumption support, so the S&P 500 should post positive returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises over 2 months and UNRATE falls over the same interval
failure_condition: Reject if joint payroll gains and falling unemployment do not produce a positive 20d return edge, or if the effect is absent when PAYEMS is the only improving leg.

mechanism_narrative: In a manufacturing contraction regime, a rebound in INDPRO signals inventory rebuilding and improving cyclical output, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO turns up from a 2-month decline while HOUST is still below its recent average
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not positive after INDPRO stabilization in contraction regimes, or if the signal fails when housing remains weak.
